content.full_text:
Assembly California Legislature

ROSS JOHNSON
ASSEMBLYMAN, SIXTY-FOURTH DISTRICT
ASSEMBLY REPUBLICAN LEADER

March 27, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Scott:

In recent telephone advice, you indicated that an elected official or candidate cannot contribute campaign funds to a political party for generic voter registration or a generic absentee ballot program if the funds are earmarked for a specific legislative district. I would appreciate written confirmation on that advice and would also appreciate your advice on the following questions.

Can an elected official or candidate contribute campaign funds to a political party for generic voter registration if the funds are earmarked for any specific legislative district, city council district, etc.?

Can an elected official or candidate contribute campaign funds to a political party for generic absentee ballot programs if those funds are not earmarked for any specific legislative district, city council district, etc.?

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]
ROSS JOHNSON
Assembly Republican Leader
